DARRYL R. WOLD
ATTO R N EY AI LAW
3I5 ] AI RWAY
COSTA MESA. CALIFORNIA 92626
17t4l 64t-4942 
FAX (7r4) s46-rO03
$r.'i.
' .,;
January 21, L99l
Jonathan Rothman, Esq.
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Conmission
42a J. Street, suit-e 8oo
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 
958 o4 -08 07
Re: Request For Written Advice
Dear Jonathan:
This letter follows up on our telephone conversation on
December 20, L99o, and requests written advice from the
Commission pursuant to covernment code S83114(b).
I am authorized to make this request on behalf of my
clients: 
Brad Gates, an incumbent officeholder as Sheriff of
Orange County; Friends of Brad Gates, his controlled carnpaign
committee (the "cornmittee"); and David R. white rr, cPA, the
committee I s treasurer.
In our telephone conversatj-on of December 20, I requested
advice concerning the application of the provi.sions of chapter 5,
Article 8 of the Political 
Reform Act, and in particular the
provisions concerning the use of campaign funds to nake gifts, to
a particular factual situation. 
(These provisions have now been
repeaLed and re-enacted under different numbers, but apparently
without substantive change that would affect the advice you gave
rne in our telephone conversation.)
The fact situation is this: 
The Conmittee wishes to hol-d a
fund-raising event, probably a reception with hosted cocktails
and hors droeuvres. It will ask for contributions of $1,OOO per
person to attend the event. The solicitation to potential
contributors will publicize the Committeets intention to give a
gtift to each contributor of $1,OOO for this event, ana wiit
describe the gift in some detail . The Connittee feels that the
nature of the gift wi.1I provide at least sorne incentive to
potential contributors to make the contribution. 
(Contributors
of 91,00o who cannot attend the event wouLd nevertheless be given
the gift. )


Jonathan Rothman, Esq.
January 2l , L99L
Page Two
The gift hrill be a western style belt buckle, made of solid
sterling silver and featuring a bust of the I j-keness of John
wayne desigtned and created for the buckle by an artist
commissioned for this purpose. John Wayne's likeness wj-11- be used
by perrnission of Wayne Enterprises, holders of exclusive rights
to the use of his likeness, without charge to the committee.
The buckles will be produced in a numbered lirnj,ted edj-tion, in a
quantity needed for this event. The cost to the committee of the
buckles, including for design, naterial-s, production, and
packaging, will be approximately $230 per buckle. (This figure
does not includ.e the value of +,he perrnission +-c use the l-ikeness
of John wayne, which w111 be donated to the committee and which
is at present undetermined. )
The Comrnittee does not anticipate personalizing the buckles.
The conmittee has not made any determination as to the value of
the individual- buckles. For those reasons, the committee does
not anticipate that the buckl-es would be excluded from the
definition of gifts by S82028(b) (6).
You suggested i-n our telephone conversation that it appeared
that the gifts of the buckles would be directly rel-ated to a
political 
purpose, and would therefor not be prohibited by the
provisions of the Act. 
You also reconmended, however, that the
Cornroittee obtain lrritten advice on this issue before proceeding.
This letter therefor requests srritten advice whether the gift of
the buckl-es as proposed in this letter would be prohibited by the
Act.
If you need any
please let me know.
this natter.
further information before respondinq,
Thank you very nuch for your assistance in

sections.question: This letter therefor requests srritten advice whether the gift of the buckles as proposed in this letter would be prohibited by the Act.
sections.question_synthetic: Are campaign committee gifts of sterling silver belt buckles featuring John Wayne's likeness, costing approximately $230 per buckle and given to $1,000 contributors, prohibited under the Political Reform Act's provisions concerning use of campaign funds for gifts?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal CONCLUSION or QUESTION section headers present. The question is identified from the final paragraph. The letter references a prior telephone conversation and requests written advice pursuant to Government Code §83114(b). Document contains detailed factual scenario about proposed campaign gift.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: This letter therefor requests srritten advice whether the gift of the buckles as proposed in this letter would be prohibited by the Act.

embedding.summary: Attorney Darryl Wold requests written advice from the FPPC on behalf of Brad Gates (Orange County Sheriff), his campaign committee, and the committee treasurer regarding whether the committee may gift custom sterling silver belt buckles (costing ~$230 each and featuring John Wayne's likeness) to contributors of $1,000 to a fundraising event.
